channel.title: Authentication Protocols & Services on Heimdall
channel.description: Recent content in Authentication Protocols & Services on Heimdall

item.title: First-Party Authentication with OpenID Connect
item.description:
By the end of this guide, you’ll have a functional setup where heimdall uses Keycloak to authenticate users and route requests based on their authentication status and roles for role-based access control.

Although this guide uses Keycloak as identity provider (IDP), you can achieve the same results with Zitadel, Boruta, or any other OpenID Connect compatible IDP.

Overview

In this guide, we’ll set up a Docker Compose environment where heimdall secures services and controls access for specific endpoints:

Introduction

Model Context Protocol (MCP) makes it easy to expose tools, resources, and prompts to agentic systems. That is powerful, but it also turns MCP servers into security-sensitive HTTP endpoints: an MCP client can discover available capabilities and invoke tools that may read data, create tickets, or trigger operational actions.

This guide shows how to protect a remote MCP server with heimdall. Instead of implementing authentication, authorization, JSON-RPC-aware error handling, and upstream identity handling inside the MCP server, you place heimdall in front of it. heimdall validates the caller, authorizes JSON-RPC methods and MCP tool calls, and forwards a trusted upstream identity to the MCP server.

This guide explains how to protect a remote MCP server with heimdall.

By the end of this guide, you’ll have a local setup where MCP Inspector connects to a FastMCP server through heimdall. heimdall validates client access tokens, authorizes MCP tool calls based on roles, returns JSON-RPC-compatible error responses, and forwards a heimdall-issued JWT to the MCP server.

Overview

The setup consists of four components:

Component Purpose

MCP Inspector

Browser-based MCP client used to connect to the protected MCP endpoint and call tools.

heimdall

Runs in proxy mode and protects the MCP server.

FastMCP demo server

Provides a small MCP server with tools requiring different roles.

Token helper

Issues local demo access tokens for viewer, operator, and admin users.

The request flow is:
